meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve can't scale projects (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't scale projects. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Antigravity Template to Scale Your SaaS Apps
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with scaling. Learn how the Antigravity project template and Claude Code help you build production-ready apps as a student developer.
content.answer_block: The Antigravity project template solves scaling bottlenecks by enforcing modular, production-grade architecture from line one. By integrating Claude Code, you automate boilerplate and complex refactoring, allowing you to focus on logic. This combination transforms your student projects into professional-grade SaaS applications ready for real-world deployment and high-concurrency traffic.
content.intro:
You have spent weeks coding, only to hit the 'spaghetti wall'—where every new feature breaks three others and your codebase feels like a sinking ship. This is the moment most student projects die, not because you lack talent, but because you lack a scalable foundation. Scaling isn't just about servers; it is about the architecture you choose on day one.

Enter the Antigravity project template. Think of it as a pre-flight checklist for your code, providing a hardened structure that handles complexity before it starts. Paired with Claude Code, you are no longer just a student writing scripts; you are an architect orchestrating a scalable machine.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Environment
steps.description: Establish the root structure that enforces strict separation of concerns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the Antigravity modular pattern, ensuring strict separation between API services, database models, and client-side state management.

steps.title: Define Scalable Data Schemas
steps.description: Use Antigravity's schema definitions to prevent future database migrations that break your app.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a scalable schema for a multi-tenant SaaS application, focusing on indexing strategies for high-read scenarios.

steps.title: Implement Modular Service Layers
steps.description: Decouple your business logic from the HTTP layer to ensure your code remains testable as it grows.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the current logic into a service layer pattern that handles business rules independently of the API request/response cycle.

steps.title: Integrate Global Error Handling
steps.description: Stop manual try-catch blocks and implement a centralized error pipeline.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a global error handling middleware that logs to an external service while returning user-friendly messages.

steps.title: Automate Testing Cycles
steps.description: Ensure your project stays stable with automated unit and integration tests.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a comprehensive test suite for the newly created service layers using Jest and Supertest.

pro_tips: Always keep your business logic in 'use-case' files; never let the API router do the heavy lifting.
Use Antigravity's dependency injection container to mock external APIs during development, saving your rate limits.
When using Claude Code, provide the 'context.md' file from your Antigravity root to ensure the AI understands your project structure.
Treat your database migrations as immutable history—never edit a migration file after it has been pushed to your remote repository.
Implement feature flags early; it is the secret weapon for deploying half-finished features without breaking production.
content.next_steps: Deploy your project to a staging environment, run a load test using Artillery, and iterate on your performance metrics.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting scaling issues define your limits. Use the tools that professional architects trust to keep their code clean, fast, and ready for thousands of users. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
